w:r.w:t: HMS — VERSION 2 AUDIT
COMPLETE USER JOURNEY ANALYSIS · FEATURE GAPS · PAYMENT INTEGRATION · WHAT TO BUILD NEXT
April 2026 | Goes through every file, every user flow, every screen
1. WHAT YOU FIXED SINCE V1 — GIVE CREDIT
You made serious progress. Here is every concrete thing that improved:
1.1 Backend — ALL 12 Services Now Exist ✓

patient-service: Built from scratch. Patient entity has MRN, firstName, lastName, DOB, gender, bloodGroup, phone, email, address, emergencyContact, insuranceProvider, insurancePolicyNumber, mergedId. This was the biggest missing piece.
lab-service: LabOrder, LabTest, LabReport entities. LabController with order, result, verify, reject endpoints. Feign client to notification-service for alerts.
medical-records-service: VisitNote, VitalRecord, AllergyRecord, IcdCode, ProblemRecord entities. Full CRUD controller and service.
pharmacy-service: Prescription entity (medicationName, dose, frequency, duration, route, instructions). Medication catalog entity. Dispense workflow.

notification-service: WebSocket STOMP fully wired (SimpMessagingTemplate, JWT STOMP channel interceptor). Notification preferences (email/SMS per type). SLA escalation + auto-scheduler. Email and SMS stubs ready for real provider integration.
reporting-service: Feign clients to all 4 data services (appointment, billing, doctor, patient). Aggregates live data for admin dashboard.
1.2 Security — Critical Fixes Applied ✓
JWT secret default REMOVED. Config now reads: secret: ${HMS_JWT_SECRET} with NO fallback. App will refuse to start without it being set. This was the most dangerous bug.
JWT access token expiry: 10 hours → 15 minutes. Correct.
ADMIN registration blocked. AuthService now checks callerRole header and throws SecurityException if a non-ADMIN tries to register an ADMIN account.
RateLimiterConfig.java created with IP-based key resolver. The infrastructure is there.
1.3 Data Model — Normalized ✓
DoctorSchedule entity created: dayOfWeek, startTime, endTime. Doctor availability is no longer a free-text string.
Appointment.chiefComplaint field added. Frontend and backend are now in sync.
Flyway migrations added: V1__init.sql present in auth, appointment, doctor, patient, lab, billing, pharmacy, medical-records, notification services.
infra/mysql/init/01-hms-databases.sql present — databases auto-created on MySQL startup.
1.4 Infrastructure — Production-Ready Shift ✓
Every service now has a Dockerfile. Docker Compose builds from source instead of running mvn inside the container.
Health checks added for MySQL and Redis. Services wait for dependencies.
Redis added to Docker Compose for session/rate-limit caching.
Per-service database users: auth_user, doctor_user, billing_user, patient_user, appointment_user. Separate credentials, not all root.
1.5 Frontend — Major UI Overhaul ✓
Light theme — dark UI (#060b14) replaced with clinical white (#F8FAFC background, #FFFFFF surfaces, #1A3C6E primary navy). This is the right call for a hospital product.
Phosphor Icons integrated. Emoji navigation icons replaced with ph-calendar, ph-user, ph-pill, etc. Professional.
Idle timeout service: 15-minute inactivity timer with 60-second countdown before auto-logout. This is a HIPAA/compliance requirement.
Command palette: Ctrl+K / Cmd+K triggers a modal search overlay with grouped results.
Shared component library: autocomplete, data-table, date-picker, time-picker, file-upload, modal, mrn-display, status-badge, not-found. Real reusable components.
Billing component: Substantial. Shows invoices, outstanding/paid totals, claim transitions, admin claim management tools.
Patient portal: Now loads actual labs count, prescription count, medication timeline, recent visit summary, notifications. Not placeholder data.
2. WHAT IS STILL BROKEN OR MISSING — HONEST ASSESSMENT
2.1 Rate Limiting — Config Written But NEVER ACTUALLY ON
BUG:

In api-gateway/src/main/resources/application.yml, lines 52-53 read: #redis-rate-limiter.replenishRate: 5 and #redis-rate-limiter.burstCapacity: 10. These are COMMENTED OUT. The entire RateLimiterConfig.java bean exists, Redis is running in Docker, but the filter is never applied. Login endpoint is still wide open to brute force.
2.2 Docker Compose — 5 Services Still Missing From Compose File
The docker-compose.yml cuts off after patient-service. These services are NOT in the compose file:
notification-service — WebSocket won't be reachable
lab-service — All lab API calls return 503
medical-records-service — Records page broken
pharmacy-service — Prescriptions page broken
reporting-service — Admin analytics blank
You have built 12 services. Your compose file runs only 7 of them.
2.3 The Success-in-Error-Box Bug — STILL THERE
BUG:

In appointment-dashboard.component.ts, the successMessage() method reads: this.error = msg. So when you successfully book an appointment, the UI displays your success message inside the red error div. This was reported last time and still hasn't been fixed.
2.4 Currency — Billing Shows $ Everywhere, Not ₹
BUG:

billing.component.ts has: Outstanding: ${{ totalOutstanding }} and Paid: ${{ totalPaid }}. The service summary sidebar shows $150.00, $85.00, $45.00. This is an Indian hospital system. Every amount should be in ₹ (INR). Change $ to ₹ everywhere. Also the totalAmount in Invoice entity should validate as INR amounts.
2.5 Registration Form Collects Nothing Useful for Patients
BUG:

register.component.ts form only has 3 fields: username, password, role. A patient who registers has NO demographic data stored at registration. This means when appointment is booked, the patient profile in patient-service is empty. The patient-service entity has firstName, lastName, DOB, gender, bloodGroup, phone, email — none of this is collected at signup. The registration form needs to be role-specific: when PATIENT is selected, show full demographics form.
2.6 Command Palette Is a Fake
BUG:

In command-palette.component.ts, the performSearch() method contains: this.results = [{type: 'ACTION', title: 'New Appointment'...}, {type: 'ACTION', title: 'Register New Patient'...}]. These are 2 hardcoded static items. No matter what you type — 'Dr. Kumar', 'John patient', 'blood test' — you always get those same 2 results. The component looks like a real search but does nothing.
2.7 Doctor Dashboard Loads ALL Appointments Client-Side
BUG:

doctor-dashboard.component.ts calls appointmentApi.list() to get every appointment in the entire system, then filters client-side: items.filter(item => item.appointmentDate === today). As patient count grows to thousands, this loads MB of data on every login. It needs a server-side filter: GET /appointments?doctorId=X&date=2026-04-22.
2.8 ADMIN Still Selectable in Registration Dropdown
BUG:

In register.component.ts, the role dropdown includes: <option value='ADMIN'>Administrator</option>. The backend will reject it for unauthenticated callers. But showing it on the public registration form is wrong and confusing. Remove ADMIN from the dropdown. Let admins be created via a separate admin panel.
2.9 Register Form Has Dark Background in Light-Theme App
BUG:

In register.component.ts styles: .auth-shell { background: rgba(9, 15, 28, 0.88); }. The rest of the app is light theme (#F8FAFC). The register page still has the old dark background. It looks like a completely different application.
2.10 Hardcoded Emergency Phone Number in Patient Portal
BUG:
patient-portal.component.ts help card shows: Facilty: +91 98765 43210 (also a typo — 'Facilty' not 'Facility'). This is a placeholder. Either make it configurable or remove it.
3. THE USER JOURNEY — WHAT EVERY USER EXPERIENCES AND WHAT'S MISSING
This section goes through every role's complete journey from the moment they open the app. This is the core of your request. Think of yourself as the user.
3.1 PATIENT JOURNEY — Step by Step
Step 1: First Visit / Registration
What exists: Username + password + role dropdown. Submit. Account created. Redirected to patient portal.
What is MISSING at Registration
Name collection. The patient is registered with a username like 'john123'. No first name, no last name. When appointments are booked, the doctor sees 'Patient #5' with no name.
Date of birth. Age-based care is critical. A pediatrician needs to know the patient is 7 years old.
Gender. Required for clinical relevance.
Phone number. Without this, no SMS reminders.
Blood group. Emergency use.
Emergency contact. Who to call if patient is incapacitated.
Profile photo. Optional but reduces misidentification.
No email verification. Account is created and immediately active with no confirmation.
No terms & conditions / consent form at registration.

SOLUTION: After selecting role=PATIENT, show a Step 2 form: First Name, Last Name, DOB, Gender, Phone, Email, Blood Group, Emergency Contact Name + Phone. This data goes to patient-service to create the patient profile linked to the auth user ID.
Step 2: Booking an Appointment
What exists: Search doctors by name/specialty → filter by chip → select doctor → pick date → pick time slot → enter chief complaint → click 'Confirm & Schedule Appointment'. Appointment is created.
What is MISSING in Appointment Booking

PAYMENT AT BOOKING TIME. This is the single biggest missing feature. In India, hospitals collect consultation fee at the time of online booking. The patient books a slot and pays ₹500 (or whatever the consultation fee is) right there. Without payment, there is no commitment. No-show rate is 30-40% without it.

RuPay / UPI / Razorpay integration. After clicking 'Confirm & Schedule', a payment modal should appear showing: Doctor Name, Consultation Type, Date/Time, Fee Amount in ₹, Payment methods (UPI, Card, Net Banking, RuPay). On payment success → appointment confirmed. On payment failure → slot released.
Appointment TYPE selection. Is this an OPD visit? A follow-up? An emergency? A telemedicine session? Each has a different fee and different workflow.

Doctor profile card. When patient clicks a doctor in the list, there should be a panel showing: photo, qualifications, years of experience, sub-specialties, ratings/reviews, consultation fee, languages spoken. Currently it's just a name and specialty.
Doctor ratings and reviews from past patients.
Estimated wait time. If a doctor's slot is at 11:00 AM and previous patients are running late, show estimated actual consultation time.

Booking confirmation. After successful booking, a confirmation screen should appear with a booking reference number, appointment details, and options to: Add to Calendar, Download PDF confirmation, Share via WhatsApp.
SMS + Email confirmation. Currently notification-service has stubs but they just println to console. Nothing is actually sent.
Step 3: Before the Appointment
What is MISSING Pre-Visit
24-hour SMS reminder. Patient gets an SMS 24 hours before: 'Your appointment with Dr. Kumar is tomorrow at 11:00 AM at City Hospital. Reply CANCEL to cancel.'
1-hour SMS/push reminder.
Health questionnaire. Some hospitals send a pre-visit form to fill: current symptoms, medications, reason for visit. This saves consultation time.
Maps integration. Hospital location with Google Maps link.
Documents checklist. 'Please bring: previous reports, insurance card, government ID.'
Step 4: At the Hospital — Patient Check-In
What is MISSING at Check-In
Queue management. Patient arrives and gets a token number. 'You are #4 in queue for Dr. Kumar. Current wait: 25 mins.' Digital token display.
Self check-in kiosk mode. Patient scans QR code from confirmation SMS → check-in complete.
Vitals entry by nurse before doctor. Nurse enters BP, temperature, weight, SpO2 in the app. Doctor sees these before walking in.
Waiting room display. TV display showing current token being called.
Step 5: During Consultation (Doctor's Side — See Section 3.2)
Step 6: After Consultation
What is MISSING Post-Visit
Discharge summary download. After visit is marked COMPLETED, patient can download a PDF: Visit date, Doctor name, Diagnosis code, Treatment given, Follow-up instructions, Medications prescribed.
Prescription download as PDF. Patient views their prescription and downloads it to show at pharmacy.
Follow-up appointment booking. After visit completed, prompt: 'Would you like to schedule a follow-up in 2 weeks?'
Feedback / rating. After visit: 'Rate your experience with Dr. Kumar. How was your wait time? How was the consultation?'
Lab result notification. When lab results are uploaded by the lab technician, patient gets instant notification and can view results in app.
Bill settlement. After visit, outstanding invoice appears in patient billing with 'Pay Now' button that opens actual payment gateway.
Step 7: Patient Billing & Payment
Current state: Billing component shows invoices with a 'Settle Now' button. Clicking it calls payInvoice() API which internally marks the invoice PAID. THERE IS NO ACTUAL PAYMENT. No rupee moves.
What the Complete Payment Flow Must Look Like
Patient clicks 'Pay Now' on an invoice.
App opens a payment modal showing: Invoice number, Itemized charges (Consultation ₹500 + Lab ₹850 + Pharmacy ₹300 = Total ₹1650), Applicable GST (5% health service), Grand total in ₹.
Payment method selection: UPI (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm QR), Card (Visa, Mastercard, RuPay), Net Banking, EMI for large amounts.
Integration: Razorpay is the standard in India. Also supports RuPay cards natively. Cashfree and PayU are alternatives.
On payment success: Razorpay sends a webhook to billing-service with payment_id and order_id. Billing service verifies signature, marks invoice PAID, stores payment_reference. Patient gets receipt.
On payment failure: Clear error message, option to retry.
GST invoice with hospital GSTIN for claiming tax.
Insurance claim flow: Patient can submit insurance claim from app. Upload insurance card photo, policy number pre-filled from profile.
Step 8: Lab Results
What is MISSING in Lab Results for Patient
Patient can see their lab reports in the app. Currently lab component exists as stub.
Reference ranges shown alongside results. 'Hemoglobin: 11.2 g/dL (Normal: 12-16)' with visual indicator.
Critical value alert. If result is critical, red banner in app + SMS immediately.
Trend charts. Blood glucose over last 6 visits as a line chart.
Share report. Patient can share PDF report with another doctor or family member.
Lab report PDF download with hospital letterhead.
3.2 DOCTOR JOURNEY — Step by Step
Step 1: Morning Login — Doctor Dashboard
What exists: Welcome card, patient search, today's appointments list, alerts widget, quick action cards (Records/Lab/Pharmacy/Schedule).
What is MISSING on Doctor Dashboard
Calendar view for the day's appointments. Not just a list — a timeline view from 9 AM to 5 PM with blocks for each appointment. Like Google Calendar.
Today's stats: Total scheduled, Completed, Pending, No-shows.
Critical alerts at top: Unverified critical lab results, Pending prescriptions, Escalated notifications.
Pending tasks widget: 'You have 3 unsigned visit notes, 2 lab orders awaiting review.'
Schedule availability toggle. 'I am available today / Mark as on leave today.'
Step 2: Starting a Consultation
What exists: Doctor clicks appointment in agenda, sees patient ID and chief complaint. Clicks 'Begin Session' which sets patient context and navigates to /records.
What is MISSING in Consultation Workflow

Patient summary card shown automatically when session begins: Name (not just ID #5), Photo, Age, Gender, Blood Group, Known allergies, Active medications, Last visit summary — all at a glance before doctor starts talking.
Vitals entry. Doctor or nurse enters: BP (systolic/diastolic), Heart rate, Temperature, Weight, Height, SpO2. These are stored per visit.

SOAP Note editor. The core of a clinical consultation: Subjective (patient's complaint), Objective (examination findings), Assessment (diagnosis with ICD-10 code), Plan (treatment, medications, follow-up). Currently no such form exists.
ICD-10 diagnosis autocomplete. Doctor types 'diabetes' and gets: E11 Type 2 diabetes mellitus, E10 Type 1 diabetes mellitus, etc. The IcdCode entity exists but has no data loaded.
Quick prescription writing within consultation. Without leaving the page, doctor selects a drug from formulary, sets dose/frequency/duration. Prescription is created and linked to the visit.
Step 3: Ordering Lab Tests
What exists: Lab component in doctor's nav. Frontend lab-api.service.ts exists.
What is MISSING in Lab Ordering
Test catalog. Doctor types 'CBC' and gets: 'Complete Blood Count — ₹350, Results in 4 hours.' Select and order in one click.
LOINC codes. International standard for lab tests. LabOrder entity has testId but the LabTest catalog has no LOINC data.
Urgent flag. 'STAT order' for critical cases.
Order status tracking. Doctor can see: Sample collected → Processing → Resulted for each order.
Critical value callback. When a critical result comes in, push notification to doctor's screen immediately.
Lab order linked to visit. All orders for a visit are grouped together for billing.
Step 4: Writing Prescription
What exists: Pharmacy component with Prescription entity (medicationName, dose, frequency, duration, route, instructions).
What is MISSING in Prescription Writing
Drug formulary catalog. Doctor should type a drug name and see: Brand name, Generic name, Available strengths, Cost in ₹.
Drug-drug interaction check. If patient is on Warfarin and doctor prescribes Aspirin, show a warning: 'Potential major interaction detected.'
Drug-allergy check. Patient's allergy list checked against prescription.
Printable e-prescription. A formatted PDF with: Hospital letterhead, Doctor name + registration number, Patient name + MRN, Date, Each drug with dose/frequency/duration, Doctor digital signature.
Multi-drug prescription. Currently only one drug per Prescription entity. Real prescriptions have 3-8 drugs.
Pharmacy routing. After doctor saves, prescription appears in pharmacy queue for dispensing.
Step 5: Completing the Visit
What is MISSING at Visit Completion
Visit summary with one click. 'Complete Visit' button generates: Diagnosis summary, Lab orders placed, Prescriptions written, Follow-up date.
Billing trigger. When visit completed, billing-service should automatically create a charge capture event with: Consultation fee + Lab fees + Pharmacy charges. Currently billing is entirely manual.
Referral letter. If referring to specialist, generate a referral note PDF.
Discharge instructions. Printable/SMS-able patient instructions.
No-show marking. If patient doesn't show up, doctor marks NO_SHOW. This status doesn't exist in current Appointment entity.
Appointment status is only: BOOKED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED. Missing: SCHEDULED, CHECKED_IN, IN_PROGRESS, NO_SHOW.
3.3 ADMIN JOURNEY — Step by Step
Step 1: Admin Dashboard
What exists: 4 stat cards (Total Patients, Active Doctors, Today Appointments, Today Revenue) from reporting-service. Department load list.
What is MISSING on Admin Dashboard
Live charts. Revenue trend (line chart last 30 days), Appointment volume (bar chart by department), Bed occupancy (if IPD module exists).
Real-time KPIs: Current patients in hospital, Doctors on duty, Pending lab reports, Overdue invoices.
Alerts panel: Services that are down (show notification-service status, etc.), Critical unresolved escalations.
Revenue breakdown in ₹ with GST split.
Today's schedule overview by department.
Quick actions: Register a new doctor, Create a manual invoice, Send system-wide notification.
Step 2: Doctor Management
What is MISSING in Doctor Management for Admin
No admin UI to add a new doctor. Admin must use the API directly (Swagger or curl). There should be a 'Add Doctor' form in the admin panel.
Doctor schedule management. Admin sets which days/hours each doctor is available.
Doctor leave management. Mark doctor on leave, redirect their patients.
Staff credential tracking. License number, renewal date, specialization certificates.
Performance dashboard per doctor: Average appointment duration, Patient satisfaction score, No-show rate.
Step 3: Reports & Analytics
What is MISSING in Analytics
Revenue report: Daily/weekly/monthly ₹ revenue by department, doctor, service type. Export to Excel.
Appointment analytics: Volume by day of week, peak hours, specialty demand.
Patient demographics report: Age distribution, gender split, top diagnoses by ICD-10 code.
Lab turnaround time: Average time from order to result by test type.
Inventory consumption report: Which medications dispensed most, reorder alerts.
Insurance claim analytics: Approval rate, average settlement time, rejection reasons breakdown.
4. PAYMENT INTEGRATION — RAZORPAY / RUPAY COMPLETE GUIDE
This is the most important feature you asked about. Here is the complete implementation plan.
4.1 Why Payment at Booking Time is Non-Negotiable
No-show rate for free bookings in India is 35-45%. When patients pay upfront, it drops to under 10%.
Cash collection at counter is a bottleneck. Online prepayment removes queues at billing desk.
Insurance pre-authorization requires knowing payment amounts upfront.
RuPay is India's national card network. NPCI mandates all hospital payment systems support RuPay.
4.2 What to Integrate: Razorpay (Best for India)
Why Razorpay
Supports: UPI (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm, BHIM), RuPay cards, Visa/MC debit + credit, Net banking (50+ banks), Wallets, EMI
Webhook system for payment status. Bilateral verification. Most critical.
Test mode available. No real money needed for development.
SDKs: Java server-side SDK + Angular/JS frontend SDK.
GST invoice generation built in.
Settlement in T+2 (money in your bank in 2 days).
4.3 The Complete Payment Flow (Step by Step Code Architecture)
Step 1 — Frontend: Patient Clicks 'Book & Pay'
In appointment-dashboard.component.ts, replace the current book() method with bookWithPayment():
Frontend Flow
1. Patient fills booking form (doctor, date, slot, chief complaint).
2. Patient clicks 'Confirm & Pay ₹500'.
3. Angular calls your backend: POST /billing/create-payment-order with: {amount: 50000, currency: 'INR', appointmentDetails: {...}}. Note: Razorpay amounts are in paise (₹500 = 50000).
4. Backend creates Razorpay order via Razorpay Java SDK, returns {razorpayOrderId, amount, currency}.
5. Angular opens Razorpay checkout modal using their JavaScript SDK.
6. Patient completes payment (UPI/Card/etc.).
7. Razorpay calls your Angular success handler with {razorpay_payment_id, razorpay_order_id, razorpay_signature}.
8. Angular sends these 3 values to your backend: POST /billing/verify-payment.
9. Backend verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature using your Razorpay Key Secret. This prevents fraud.
10. If verified: Backend marks payment success, creates appointment, sends confirmation notification.
11. Angular navigates to booking confirmation page with reference number.
Step 2 — Backend: New billing-service Endpoints Needed
Endpoint
Method
What It Does
/billing/create-payment-order
POST
Calls Razorpay API to create an order. Returns razorpayOrderId. Add Razorpay Java SDK dependency to billing-service pom.xml.
/billing/verify-payment
POST
Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature: SHA256(orderId + '|' + paymentId, razorpayKeySecret). If valid, marks invoice PAID and creates appointment.
/billing/webhook
POST
Razorpay calls this on payment events. Verify webhook signature. Handle: payment.captured, payment.failed, refund.processed.
/billing/refund/{paymentId}
POST
Initiate refund via Razorpay API. Needed when doctor cancels appointment.
Step 3 — pom.xml Addition for billing-service
Add to billing-service/pom.xml
<dependency> <groupId>com.razorpay</groupId> <artifactId>razorpay-java</artifactId> <version>1.4.5</version> </dependency>
Then in application.yml: razorpay.key.id: ${RAZORPAY_KEY_ID} and razorpay.key.secret: ${RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET}
Add these to docker-compose.yml environment for billing-service.
Step 4 — Frontend: Install Razorpay JS SDK
Frontend Integration
In index.html: <script src='https://checkout.razorpay.com/v1/checkout.js'></script>

In appointment-dashboard.component.ts, after getting razorpayOrderId from backend, call: const rzp = new (window as any).Razorpay({ key: environment.razorpayKeyId, amount: orderResponse.amount, currency: 'INR', name: 'City Hospital', description: 'Consultation Fee', order_id: orderResponse.razorpayOrderId, handler: (response) => this.onPaymentSuccess(response), prefill: { name: patientName, email: patientEmail, contact: patientPhone }, theme: { color: '#1A3C6E' } }); rzp.open();
Add to environment.ts: razorpayKeyId: 'rzp_test_XXXXXXXX' (test key from Razorpay dashboard).
Use rzp_test_* for dev, rzp_live_* for production.
Step 5 — GST Invoice Generation
GST Requirements for Healthcare Billing
Healthcare services (consultation) are EXEMPT from GST in India under exemption list.
But diagnostic services (lab tests) attract 5% GST in some cases.
Pharmacy (drug sales) attract 12% or 18% GST based on drug type.
Generate GST invoice with: Hospital name + GSTIN, Patient name + GSTN (if applicable), HSN/SAC code for each line item, CGST + SGST or IGST breakdown.
iTextPDF or Apache PDFBox can generate invoices server-side in billing-service.
Store PDF in MinIO file store. Email link to patient.
4.4 Additional Indian Payment Context
RuPay specific: Razorpay supports RuPay debit cards natively. No extra config needed — it appears automatically in the checkout modal.
UPI Integration: When patient selects UPI, they enter their VPA (e.g., john@oksbi). Razorpay handles the collection request to their UPI app.
PhonePe / GPay / Paytm: These are UPI apps. They all work through the same UPI integration in Razorpay.
Bharat QR Code: For walk-in patients who want to pay at counter, generate a QR code from Razorpay that works with all UPI apps.
EMI for large bills: For bills above ₹3000, offer EMI. Razorpay has 15+ bank EMI options built in.
Insurance partial payment: Patient pays co-pay amount. Insurance claim filed for remainder. Show both amounts clearly.
Refund SLA: Razorpay refunds process in 5-7 business days. Show this to patient in the refund confirmation.
5. COMPLETE FEATURE LIST — EVERYTHING THAT SHOULD EXIST
Organized by what a user needs, not by technical module.
5.1 Features That EXIST But Are INCOMPLETE
Feature
Current State
What's Missing
Appointment Booking
Slot selection works
Payment at booking, appointment type, doctor profile, confirmation email/SMS
Billing
Invoice display, claim transitions
Real payment gateway, ₹ currency, GST, itemized charges, PDF invoice
Lab Results
Entities + API stubs
Test catalog with LOINC, critical alerts, reference ranges, trend charts, PDF download
Prescriptions
Single drug entity
Multi-drug, drug catalog, interaction check, printable PDF, formulary
Medical Records
VisitNote, VitalRecord
SOAP note editor, ICD-10 autocomplete with data, visit PDF export
Notifications
WebSocket + stubs
Real SMS (Twilio/MSG91), real email (SendGrid), appointment reminders scheduled
Command Palette
UI exists
Actually searches patients, doctors, appointments via API
Patient Profile
Basic entity
Profile photo upload, full demographics form, family members management
Analytics
Reporting service built
No charts on frontend, no data visualization, no Excel export
5.2 Features That Are COMPLETELY ABSENT
Missing Feature
Priority
User Impact
Payment Gateway (Razorpay/UPI/RuPay)
CRITICAL
Patients cannot actually pay. Core revenue flow is fake.
Appointment SMS/Email Reminders
CRITICAL
30-40% no-show rate without reminders.
Doctor Calendar View
HIGH
Doctors cannot visualize their day. List is insufficient.
Appointment Cancellation UI
HIGH
Patient cannot cancel from app. Must call the hospital.
Appointment Rescheduling UI
HIGH
Cannot move appointment to another slot.
Patient Self-Registration Demographics
HIGH
Patient profile is empty after registration.
Doctor Profile Card in Booking
HIGH
Patient has no info to choose between doctors.
Visit Vitals Entry (Nurse/Doctor)
HIGH
BP, temp, weight not recordable via UI.
SOAP Note Editor
HIGH
Doctors cannot write clinical notes in the app.
Prescription PDF Download
HIGH
Patients cannot take their prescription to pharmacy.
Discharge Summary PDF
HIGH
No visit summary document generated.
Post-Visit Feedback / Rating
MEDIUM
No way to rate doctor or visit quality.
ICD-10 Code Database
MEDIUM
Entity exists but no codes loaded. Diagnosis autocomplete is empty.
Drug Formulary Database
MEDIUM
No drug catalog. Prescriptions require manual drug name entry.
Telemedicine / Video Call
MEDIUM
No video consultation option.
Lab Result Trend Charts
MEDIUM
No visualization of test results over time.
Queue Token System
MEDIUM
Patients don't know their wait time.
Family Member Management
MEDIUM
Cannot book appointment for dependent/child.
Notification Preferences UI
LOW
Backend preferences exist, no frontend settings page.
Forgot Password Flow
LOW
No password reset mechanism.
Two-Factor Authentication
LOW
OTP on login for high-security roles.
Bed / Ward Management (IPD)
LOW
Inpatient admission not supported.
Admin Doctor Management UI
LOW
Admin cannot add/edit doctors from UI.
6. UPDATED SCORE CARD — V1 vs V2
Dimension
V1 Status
V2 Status
All 12 Services Built
JWT Secret Hardened
MISSING ✗
FIXED ✓
Token Expiry (15 min)
10 hours ✗
15 min ✓
Admin Reg. Blocked
MISSING ✗
FIXED ✓
Rate Limiting Active
MISSING ✗
Config only ⚠
Docker Compose Complete
12 defined ✗
Only 7 running ✗
Flyway Migrations
None ✗
All services ✓
Clinical Light Theme
Dark theme ✗
Light theme ✓
Payment Gateway (₹)
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
Appointment Reminders
MISSING ✗
Stubs only ⚠
Doctor Profile at Booking
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
Post-Visit PDF Discharge
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
SOAP Note Editor
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
ICD-10 Data Loaded
MISSING ✗
Entity only ⚠
Prescription PDF
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
Appointment Cancellation UI
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
Doctor Calendar View
MISSING ✗
Still missing ✗
Idle Timeout Service
MISSING ✗
BUILT ✓
Shared Component Library
None ✗
8 components ✓
Success-via-Error Bug
Present ✗
Still present ✗
V1 Score: 28/100 →
V2 Score: 54/100
Major architectural progress. But the user-facing features that make a hospital system actually usable by patients — payment, reminders, PDFs, cancellation, clinical notes — are still mostly absent.
7. WHAT TO BUILD NEXT — PRIORITIZED
7.1 This Week — Fix the Obvious Bugs First (2-3 days)
Uncomment rate limiting in api-gateway/application.yml. Lines 52-53: remove the # characters. Also add to application.yml: spring.data.redis.host: ${HMS_REDIS_HOST:localhost}. Done.
Fix success-via-error in appointment-dashboard.component.ts. Change successMessage() to: this.successMsg = msg (use a new property). In template add a separate green success div.
Change all $ to ₹ in billing.component.ts. Global find & replace.
Fix register form background: .auth-shell { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); }. Remove the dark rgba.
Remove ADMIN from register dropdown. Delete <option value='ADMIN'>Administrator</option> from register.component.ts.
Fix 'Facilty' typo to 'Facility' in patient-portal.component.ts.

Add the 5 missing services to docker-compose.yml: notification-service, lab-service, medical-records-service, pharmacy-service, reporting-service with their build contexts and DB environment variables.
Fix doctor dashboard API call: change appointmentApi.list() to a filtered call by doctor ID and date. Add endpoint: GET /appointments?doctorId={id}&date={date} to appointment-service.
7.2 Next 2 Weeks — Razorpay Payment Integration
Add Razorpay Java SDK to billing-service pom.xml. Configure razorpay.key.id and razorpay.key.secret in application.yml.
Add to billing-service: POST /billing/create-payment-order endpoint. Creates Razorpay order for consultation fee. Returns orderId + amount.
Add to billing-service: POST /billing/verify-payment endpoint. Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature. On success: creates appointment + marks invoice PAID.
Add to billing-service: POST /billing/webhook endpoint. Handles Razorpay payment.captured, payment.failed events.

In appointment-dashboard.component.ts: Add Razorpay checkout script in index.html. Replace book() with bookWithPayment(). After form filled, call create-payment-order, open Razorpay modal, on success call verify-payment.
Add to environment.ts: razorpayKeyId. Add to billing component: 'Pay ₹X' button with proper INR amounts.
Test full flow: Book appointment → Pay with test UPI (success@razorpay) → Appointment confirmed → Check billing shows PAID.
7.3 Following 2 Weeks — Patient-Facing Completion

Patient Registration Step 2: After account creation (username+password), redirect to /patient/complete-profile form. Collect: firstName, lastName, DOB, gender, phone, email, bloodGroup, emergencyContactName, emergencyContactPhone. POST to patient-service /patients to create profile linked to userId.
Appointment cancellation: Add Cancel button on patient's appointment card. Call PUT /appointments/{id}/status with {status: 'CANCELLED', reason: '...'}. Show confirmation dialog first.

Appointment rescheduling: 'Reschedule' button that opens the slot picker for the same doctor. When new slot selected, old appointment cancelled and new one created (with payment difference handled if applicable).

Doctor profile card: When patient clicks a doctor in booking, expand a panel showing: specialization, availability, consultation fee, rating stars, total patients. Add consultationFee field to Doctor entity.

Notification preferences page: /patient/notification-settings. Toggle switches for email appointment confirmation, SMS reminders, lab result alerts. Call notification-service PUT /notifications/preferences/{userId}.
SMS integration: In notification-service, replace sendSmsStub() with actual Twilio or MSG91 API call. Configure TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE in application.yml.
7.4 Month 2 — Clinical Features
SOAP Note editor component in Angular. Four text areas: Subjective, Objective, Assessment (with ICD-10 autocomplete), Plan. POST to medical-records-service /records/visits/{visitId}/notes.

Load ICD-10 codes into database. Download WHO ICD-10 codes CSV (free, public domain). Write a Flyway migration or data seed script to load the ~14,000 codes into icd_codes table. Enable autocomplete in SOAP editor.
Vitals entry form in doctor dashboard: Small form that appears during active consultation. Enter: BP Systolic/Diastolic, HR, Temperature, Weight, Height, SpO2. POST to /records/vitals.

Prescription PDF generation: In pharmacy-service, use iTextPDF to generate a formatted prescription PDF. Hospital letterhead, doctor details, patient details, drug list with doses. Store in MinIO. Return PDF URL.
Lab report template: In lab-service, generate HTML report when results are verified. Convert to PDF. Send notification to patient with link.
Discharge summary: When appointment status set to COMPLETED, trigger generation of discharge summary PDF: Diagnosis, Treatment given, Medications, Follow-up date. Email to patient.
END OF ANALYSIS — V2

The architecture is now genuinely strong. The gap between V1 and V2 is massive and commendable. The path forward is clear: Razorpay payment integration first, then complete the patient journey (cancellation, rescheduling, demographics), then clinical features (SOAP notes, prescriptions PDF). Each of these directly serves a real person using the system.
